questions.q: In which year did the British Cabinet Mission come to India?
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Mission arrived in India in 1946 to plan the transfer of power.

questions.q: The Cabinet Mission was sent by the government of which country?
questions.explanation: It was dispatched by the British government, then led by Prime Minister Clement Attlee.

questions.q: What was the main purpose of the Cabinet Mission?
options: To plan the transfer of power and a constitution-making body
questions.explanation: The mission proposed a framework for transferring power and creating a body to frame India's constitution.

questions.q: How many British members made up the Cabinet Mission?
questions.explanation: The mission consisted of three British cabinet ministers.

questions.q: Which British Prime Minister sent the Cabinet Mission to India?
questions.explanation: Clement Attlee's Labour government sent the mission in 1946.

questions.q: The Cabinet Mission proposed setting up a body to do what?
questions.explanation: It called for a constitution-making body, which became the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: What ultimately overtook and replaced the Cabinet Mission Plan?
questions.explanation: The plan was eventually overtaken by the demand for the partition of India.

questions.q: The Cabinet Mission Plan tried to keep India as one country or divide it?
options: Make it a colony of France
questions.explanation: The plan sought to keep India united while granting wide autonomy to provinces grouped together.

questions.q: The Cabinet Mission came at a time when which world war had just ended?
questions.explanation: It arrived in 1946, just after the end of the Second World War in 1945.

questions.q: Which two major Indian parties did the Cabinet Mission negotiate with?
options: The League and the Hindu Mahasabha
The Swaraj Party and the Ghadar Party
questions.explanation: The mission held talks chiefly with the Indian National Congress and the All-India Muslim League.

questions.q: Which of these was a member of the Cabinet Mission?
questions.explanation: Sir Stafford Cripps was one of the three Cabinet Mission members.

questions.q: Who led the Cabinet Mission as Secretary of State for India?
questions.explanation: Lord Pethick-Lawrence, the Secretary of State for India, headed the three-member mission.

questions.q: The third member of the Cabinet Mission, besides Pethick-Lawrence and Cripps, was who?
questions.explanation: A. V. Alexander, First Lord of the Admiralty, was the third member of the mission.

questions.q: The Cabinet Mission Plan proposed grouping provinces into how many sections?
questions.explanation: The plan grouped the provinces into three sections, labelled A, B and C.

questions.q: The Cabinet Mission rejected the demand for a fully sovereign separate state of Pakistan on what main ground?
options: It was supported by no one
It would leave non-Muslims in those areas dissatisfied and was unworkable
questions.explanation: The mission concluded a separate Pakistan was unworkable, partly because large non-Muslim populations would be unwilling to join it.

questions.q: Under the Cabinet Mission Plan, what kind of central government was envisaged?
options: A weak centre controlling only defence, foreign affairs and communications
questions.explanation: The plan proposed a weak union centre handling only defence, foreign affairs and communications, with residual powers to provinces.

questions.q: Which body was to be set up under the plan to govern in the interim?
questions.explanation: An Interim Government was to be formed from elected Indian leaders to run affairs during the transition.

questions.q: The Muslim League initially accepted the Cabinet Mission Plan but later did what?
options: Withdrew its acceptance and called for Direct Action
questions.explanation: The League first accepted the plan but withdrew acceptance and launched Direct Action Day in August 1946.

questions.q: The elections held under the Cabinet Mission framework were for which body?
questions.explanation: Provincial assemblies elected members to the Constituent Assembly under the Cabinet Mission Plan.

questions.q: Who was the Viceroy of India during the Cabinet Mission's visit in 1946?
questions.explanation: Lord Wavell was Viceroy in 1946 and worked alongside the Cabinet Mission and on forming the Interim Government.

questions.q: A central dispute that wrecked the plan concerned the rules about provinces joining which structure?
options: The compulsory grouping of provinces into sections
questions.explanation: Congress and the League clashed over whether grouping of provinces into sections was compulsory or optional, undermining the plan.

questions.q: The Cabinet Mission Plan was published in May of which year?
questions.explanation: The mission released its plan in May 1946 after weeks of negotiations in India.

questions.q: At which hill station did the Cabinet Mission hold a key conference with Indian leaders in 1946?
questions.explanation: A major conference was convened at Simla, though it failed to produce agreement between Congress and the League.

questions.q: Which statement by Jawaharlal Nehru in July 1946 alarmed the Muslim League about the grouping clause?
options: That India would join the Commonwealth
That the army would be disbanded
That the Assembly would be free to modify the plan
questions.explanation: Nehru's remark that the Constituent Assembly would be sovereign and could alter the plan led the League to withdraw its acceptance.

questions.q: Sections B and C under the plan corresponded broadly to the Muslim-majority areas of which regions?
questions.explanation: Section B grouped north-western provinces and Section C the north-eastern ones, both Muslim-majority, while Section A held the rest.

questions.q: Under the plan, provinces were to be allowed to opt out of their assigned section after what interval?
options: After the first general elections under the new constitution
questions.explanation: Provinces could reconsider and leave their grouping after the first elections under the new constitution, a much-debated provision.

questions.q: The Cabinet Mission Plan deliberately avoided creating a sovereign Pakistan but conceded what to the League?
options: Autonomous Muslim-majority groupings within a loose union
questions.explanation: Instead of partition, the plan offered Muslim-majority provinces grouped autonomy within a loose all-India union.

questions.q: The Interim Government formed in September 1946 was headed by which Congress leader as Vice-President of the Viceroy's Executive Council?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru led the Interim Government as Vice-President of the Executive Council in September 1946.

questions.q: The League's withdrawal from the Cabinet Mission Plan culminated in which violent event of August 1946?
questions.explanation: Direct Action Day on 16 August 1946 led to the Great Calcutta Killings, deepening communal division and dooming the plan.

questions.q: Why is the Cabinet Mission often described as the last serious attempt at a united India?
options: It proposed an all-India union before partition became unavoidable
questions.explanation: The plan offered a federal structure keeping India united, and its failure cleared the path to partition the following year.
